tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قل إنما أعظكم بواحدة» هي «أن تقوموا لله» أي لأجله «مثنى» أي اثنين اثنين «وفرادى» واحدا واحدا «ثم تتفكروا» فتعلموا «ما بصاحبكم» محمد «من جنة» جنون «إن» ما «هو إلا نذير لكم بين يدي» أي قبل «عذاب شديد» في الآخرة إن عصيتموه
tafsir.siraj-ar: ۞قُلۡ إِنَّمَآ أَعِظُكُم بِوَٰحِدَةٍۖ أَن تَقُومُواْ لِلَّهِ مَثۡنَىٰ وَفُرَٰدَىٰ ثُمَّ تَتَفَكَّرُواْۚ مَا بِصَاحِبِكُم مِّن جِنَّةٍۚ إِنۡ هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٞ لَّكُم بَيۡنَ يَدَيۡ عَذَابٖ شَدِيدٖ (بِوَاحِدَةٍ: بِخَصْلَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ, مَثْنَى: اثْنَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ, جِنَّةٍ: جُنُونٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah! "Sesungguhnya aku hendak memperingatkan kepada kalian suatu hal saja) yaitu (supaya kalian menghadap Allah) dengan ikhlas hanya karena-Nya (dua-dua) yakni berduaan (atau sendiri-sendiri) satu persatu (kemudian kalian pikirkan tentang -Muhammad-) sehingga kalian mengetahui (tidak ada pada diri kawan kalian ini) yakni Nabi Muhammad (penyakit gila sedikit pun) yakni kegilaan (tidak lain dia hanyalah pemberi peringatan bagi kalian sebelum) kalian menghadapi (azab yang keras.") di akhirat nanti jika kalian mendurhakainya

ar: قُلۡ مَا سَأَلۡتُكُم مِّنۡ أَجۡرࣲ فَهُوَ لَكُمۡۖ إِنۡ أَجۡرِیَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَیۡءࣲ شَهِیدࣱ
ar_simple: قل ما سألتكم من أجر فهو لكم إن أجری إلا على ٱلله وهو على كل شیء شهید

t.sahih-int: Say, "Whatever payment I might have asked of you - it is yours. My payment is only from Allah, and He is, over all things, Witness
t.asad: Say: “No reward have I ever asked of you [out of anything] that is yours: my reward rests with none but God, and He is witness unto everything!”
t.yusuf-ali: Say: "No reward do I ask of you: it is (all) in your interest: my reward is only due from Allah: And He is witness to all things
t.pickthall: Say: Whatever reward I might have asked of you is yours. My reward is the affair of Allah only. He is Witness over all things
t.arberry: Say: 'I have asked no wage of you; that shall be yours. My wage falls only upon God; and He is witness over everything
t.hilali-khan: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Whatever wage I might have asked of you is yours. My wage is from Allah only. and He is Witness over all things
t.itani: Say, “Whatever compensation I have asked of you, is yours. My compensation comes only from God, and He is Witness over all things.”
t.mubarakpuri: Say: "Whatever wage I might have asked of you is yours. My wage is from Allah only, and He is a Witness over all things."
t.qarib: Say, "The payment for which you think I ask you is yours. My payment comes only from God, and He is the Witness over all things
t.wahiduddin: Say, "If I have asked you for any recompense, you can keep it. It is God alone who will reward me: He is the witness of all things
t.shakir: Say: Whatever reward I have asked of you, that is only for yourselves; my reward is only with Allah, and He is a witness of all things
t.haleem: Say, ‘If I have asked you for any reward, you can keep it. It is God alone who will reward me: He is witness to everything.’
t.maariful: Say, “If I had ever claimed any reward from you, then it is yours. My reward is with none but Allah. And He is witness over every thing.”
t.khattab: Say, “If I had ever asked you for a reward, you could keep it. My reward is only from Allah. And He is a Witness over all things.”
t.ghali: Say, "Whatever reward I ask of you, then it is for you (i.e., for benefit and your guidance) Decidedly my reward (falls) upon none except Allah, and He is an Ever - Present Witness over everything
t.irving: SAY: "What I ask you in payment is merely something for yourselves. My own payment lies only up to God; He is a Witness for everything
t.iwq: Say, "Whatever payment I might have asked of you - it is yours. My payment is only from Allah, and He is, over all things, Witness

t_id.kemenag: Katakanlah: "Upah apapun yang aku minta kepadamu, maka itu untuk kamu. Upahku hanyalah dari Allah, dan Dia Maha Mengetahui segala sesuatu".
t_id.sabiq: Katakanlah kepada orang-orang kafir itu, "Kebaikan dari pesan-pesan suci yang kusampaikan ini sebenarnya akan terpulang kepada kalian. Tak ada suatu imbalan yang kuharapkan kecuali dari Allah. Sesungguhnya Dia Mahaawas dan Mahacermat atas segala sesuatu."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah) kepada mereka! ("Upah apa pun yang aku minta kepada kalian) atas menyampaikan peringatan dan menyampaikan risalah Rabbku (maka upah itu untuk kalian) maksudnya aku tidak meminta upah apa pun atas hal ini. (Upahku tiada lain) tidak lain upahku (hanyalah dari Allah, dan Dia Maha Mengetahui segala sesuatu") Maha Menyaksikan dan Maha Mengetahui tentang kebenaranku.
t_id.iwq: Katakanlah: "Upah apapun yang aku minta kepadamu, maka itu untuk kamu. Upahku hanyalah dari Allah, dan Dia Maha Mengetahui segala sesuatu".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قل» لهم «ما سألتكم» على الإنذار والتبليغ «من أجر فهو لكم» أي لا أسألكم عليه أجرا «إن أجري» ما ثوابي «إلا على الله وهو على كل شيء شهيد» مطلع يعلم صدقي
tafsir.siraj-ar: قُلۡ مَا سَأَلۡتُكُم مِّنۡ أَجۡرٖ فَهُوَ لَكُمۡۖ إِنۡ أَجۡرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيۡءٖ شَهِيدٞ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah) kepada mereka! ("Upah apa pun yang aku minta kepada kalian) atas menyampaikan peringatan dan menyampaikan risalah Rabbku (maka upah itu untuk kalian) maksudnya aku tidak meminta upah apa pun atas hal ini. (Upahku tiada lain) tidak lain upahku (hanyalah dari Allah, dan Dia Maha Mengetahui segala sesuatu") Maha Menyaksikan dan Maha Mengetahui tentang kebenaranku

ar: قُلۡ إِنَّ رَبِّی یَقۡذِفُ بِٱلۡحَقِّ عَلَّـٰمُ ٱلۡغُیُوبِ
ar_simple: قل إن ربی یقذف بٱلحق علم ٱلغیوب

t.sahih-int: Say, "Indeed, my Lord projects the truth. Knower of the unseen
t.asad: Say: “Verily, my Sustainer hurls the truth [against all that is false] He who fully knows all the things that are beyond the reach of a created being’s perception!”
t.yusuf-ali: Say: "Verily my Lord doth cast the (mantle of) Truth (over His servants),- He that has full knowledge of (all) that is hidden
t.pickthall: Say: Lo! my Lord hurleth the truth. (He is) the Knower of Things Hidden
t.arberry: Say: 'My Lord hurls the truth -- the Knower of the Unseen
t.hilali-khan: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Verily! My Lord sends down Inspiration and makes apparent the truth (i.e. this Revelation that had come to me), the AllKnower of the Ghaib (unseen)
t.itani: Say, “My Lord projects the truth. He is the Knower of the Unseen.”
t.mubarakpuri: Say: "Verily, my Lord sends down the truth, the All-Knower of the Unseen."
t.qarib: Say, "My Lord hurls forth the Truth, for He has the full knowledge of all that is beyond our knowledge
t.wahiduddin: Say to them, "My Lord hurls forth the Truth [at falsehood] and He is the knower of hidden things
t.shakir: Say: Surely my Lord utters the truth, the great Knower of the unseen
t.haleem: Say, ‘My Lord hurls the Truth down [before you]. He has full knowledge of all that is unseen.’
t.maariful: Say, “My Lord sends forth the Truth. He is the Best-Knower of the Unseen.”
t.khattab: Say, “Surely my Lord hurls the truth ˹against falsehood˺. ˹He is˺ the Knower of all unseen.”
t.ghali: Say, "Surely my Lord hurls the Truth (against the untruth)-The Sublime Knower of the (things) Unseen
t.irving: SAY: "My Lord, the Knower of Unseen things, aims at the Truth
t.iwq: Say, "Indeed, my Lord projects the truth. Knower of the unseen

t_id.kemenag: Katakanlah: "Sesungguhnya Tuhanku mewahyukan kebenaran. Dia Maha Mengetahui segala yang ghaib".
t_id.sabiq: Katakanlah kepada mereka, "Sesungguhnya Tuhanku telah melontarkan kebenaran ke arah kebatilan sehingga kebatilan itu musnah. Dia Maha Mengetahui semua kegaiban sehingga tidak ada satu rahasia pun yang luput dari pengawasan-Nya."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah! "Sesungguhnya Rabbku mewahyukan kebenaran) yakni menyampaikannya kepada nabi-nabi-Nya (Dia Maha Mengetahui segala yang gaib") semua apa yang gaib dari makhluk-Nya baik yang di langit maupun yang di bumi.
t_id.iwq: Katakanlah: "Sesungguhnya Tuhanku mewahyukan kebenaran. Dia Maha Mengetahui segala yang ghaib".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قل إن ربي يقذف بالحق» يلقيه إلى أنبيائه «علاًم الغيوب» ما غاب عن خلقه في السماوات والأرض
tafsir.siraj-ar: قُلۡ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَقۡذِفُ بِٱلۡحَقِّ عَلَّـٰمُ ٱلۡغُيُوبِ (يَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِّ: يَرْمِي بِحُجَجِ الحَقِّ عَلَى البَاطِلِ؛ فَيَدْمَغُهُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah! "Sesungguhnya Rabbku mewahyukan kebenaran) yakni menyampaikannya kepada nabi-nabi-Nya (Dia Maha Mengetahui segala yang gaib") semua apa yang gaib dari makhluk-Nya baik yang di langit maupun yang di bumi

ar: قُلۡ جَاۤءَ ٱلۡحَقُّ وَمَا یُبۡدِئُ ٱلۡبَـٰطِلُ وَمَا یُعِیدُ
ar_simple: قل جاء ٱلحق وما یبدئ ٱلبطل وما یعید

t.sahih-int: Say, "The truth has come, and falsehood can neither begin [anything] nor repeat [it]
t.asad: Say: “The truth has now come [to light, and falsehood is bound to wither away]: for, falsehood cannot bring forth anything new, nor can it bring back [what has passed away].”
t.yusuf-ali: Say: "The Truth has arrived, and Falsehood neither creates anything new, nor restores anything
t.pickthall: Say: The Truth hath come, and falsehood showeth not its face and will not return
t.arberry: Say. 'Truth has come; falsehood originates not, nor brings again
t.hilali-khan: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "The truth (the Quran and Allah's Inspiration) has come, and AlBatil [falsehood - Iblis (Satan)] can neither create anything nor resurrect (anything)
t.itani: Say, “The Truth has come; while falsehood can neither originate, nor regenerate.”
t.mubarakpuri: Say: "The truth has come, and the falsehood can neither create anything nor resurrect."
t.qarib: Say, "The Truth has come, and falsehood can neither originate anything nor regenerate it
t.wahiduddin: Say to them, "The Truth has come and will endure. Falsehood has no power to originate any good, nor to reproduce it
t.shakir: Say: The truth has come, and the falsehood shall vanish and shall not come back
t.haleem: Say, ‘The Truth has come; falsehood is powerless.’
t.maariful: Say, “Truth has come, and falsehood (has vanished, and it) has no power to produce or reproduce (anything).”
t.khattab: Say, “The truth has come, and falsehood will vanish, never to return.”
t.ghali: Say, "The Truth has come; and in no way (can) untruth start (anything) and in no way (can) it bring back (anything)
t.irving: SAY: "Truth has come, while falsehood neither originates nor does anything over again
t.iwq: Say, "The truth has come, and falsehood can neither begin anything nor repeat it

t_id.kemenag: Katakanlah: "Kebenaran telah datang dan yang batil itu tidak akan memulai dan tidak (pula) akan mengulangi".
t_id.sabiq: Katakanlah kepada mereka, "Islam telah muncul. Dengan kemunculannya, kebatilan tak dapat lagi dijadikan sebagai sarana untuk menolak kebenaran. Sarana-sarana sebelumnya pun tak dapat lagi melakukan hal itu."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah, "Kebenaran telah datang) yakni agama Islam (dan yang batil itu tidak akan memulai) kekafiran itu tidak akan memulai (dan tidak pula akan mengulangi") tidak akan ada bekasnya lagi.
t_id.iwq: Katakanlah: "Kebenaran telah datang dan yang batil itu tidak akan memulai dan tidak (pula) akan mengulangi".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قل جاء الحق» الإسلام «وما يبدئ الباطل» الكفر «وما يعيد» أي لم يبق له أثر
tafsir.siraj-ar: قُلۡ جَآءَ ٱلۡحَقُّ وَمَا يُبۡدِئُ ٱلۡبَٰطِلُ وَمَا يُعِيدُ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah, "Kebenaran telah datang) yakni agama Islam (dan yang batil itu tidak akan memulai) kekafiran itu tidak akan memulai (dan tidak pula akan mengulangi") tidak akan ada bekasnya lagi

ar: قُلۡ إِن ضَلَلۡتُ فَإِنَّمَاۤ أَضِلُّ عَلَىٰ نَفۡسِیۖ وَإِنِ ٱهۡتَدَیۡتُ فَبِمَا یُوحِیۤ إِلَیَّ رَبِّیۤۚ إِنَّهُۥ سَمِیعࣱ قَرِیبࣱ
ar_simple: قل إن ضللت فإنما أضل على نفسی وإن ٱهتدیت فبما یوحی إلی ربی إنه سمیع قریب

t.sahih-int: Say, "If I should err, I would only err against myself. But if I am guided, it is by what my Lord reveals to me. Indeed, He is Hearing and near
t.asad: Say: “Were I to go astray, I would but go astray [due to my own self, and] to the hurt of myself; but if I am on the right path, it is but by virtue of what my Sustainer reveals unto me: for, verily, He is all-hearing, ever-near!”
t.yusuf-ali: Say: "If I am astray, I only stray to the loss of my own soul: but if I receive guidance, it is because of the inspiration of my Lord to me: it is He Who hears all things, and is (ever) near
t.pickthall: Say: If I err, I err only to my own loss, and if I am rightly guided it is because of that which my Lord hath revealed unto me. Lo! He is Hearer, Nigh
t.arberry: Say: 'If I go astray, I go astray only to my own loss; if I am guided, it is by what my Lord reveals to me. He is All-hearing, Ever-nigh
t.hilali-khan: Say: "If (even) I go astray, I shall stray only to my own loss. But if I remain guided, it is because of the Inspiration of my Lord to me. Truly, He is AllHearer, Ever Near (to all things)
t.itani: Say, “If I err, I err only to my own loss; but if I am guided, it is by what my Lord inspires me. He is Hearing and Near.”
t.mubarakpuri: Say: "If (even) I go astray, I shall stray only to my own loss. But if I remain guided, it is because of the Revelation of my Lord to me. Truly, He is All-Hearer, Ever Near."
t.qarib: Say, "If I should be misguided, it is only to my own detriment, but if I am guided, it is through what my Lord reveals to me. He is the All-Hearing, the Near
t.wahiduddin: Affirm, "If I am in error, I shall carry the burden thereof; and if I am rightly guided, it is because of what my Lord has revealed to me. Truly, He is all-hearing and near at hand
t.shakir: Say: If I err, I err only against my own soul, and if I follow a right direction, it is because of what my Lord reveals to me; surely He is Hearing, Nigh
t.haleem: Say, ‘If I go astray, that is my loss, and if I am rightly guided, it is through what my Lord has revealed to me. He is all hearing, and ever near.’
t.maariful: Say, “If I go astray, I shall go astray only to my own detriment. And if I follow the right path, it is because of what my Lord reveals to me. Surely He is All-Hearer, Ever-Near.”
t.khattab: Say, “If I am astray, the loss is only mine. And if I am guided, it is ˹only˺ because of what my Lord reveals to me. He is indeed All-Hearing, Ever Near.”
t.ghali: Say, "In case I err, surely I will err away only against my (own) self; and in case I am guided, then it is by what my Lord reveals to me. Surely He is Ever-Hearing, Ever-Near
t.irving: SAY: "If I have strayed, I have only led myself astray; while if I have been guided, then it is because of what my Lord has inspired in me. He is Alert, so Near!" The Pursuit of Faith
t.iwq: Say, "If I should err, I would only err against myself. But if I am guided, it is by what my Lord reveals to me. Indeed, He is Hearing and near

t_id.kemenag: Katakanlah: "Jika aku sesat maka sesungguhnya aku sesat atas kemudharatan diriku sendiri; dan jika aku mendapat petunjuk maka itu adalah disebabkan apa yang diwahyukan Tuhanku kepadaku. Sesungguhnya Dia Maha Mendengar lagi Maha Dekat".
t_id.sabiq: Katakanlah kepada mereka, "Jika aku menyalahi kebenaran, maka kerugian akan menimpa diriku sendiri. Jika aku mendapatkan petunjuk, hal itu karena adanya petunjuk Tuhanku. Sesungguhnya Dia Maha Mendengar segala ucapanku dan ucapan kalian. Dia pun Mahadekat dengan diriku dan diri kalian."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah! "Jika aku sesat) dari kebenaran (maka sesungguhnya aku sesat atas kemudaratan diriku sendiri) yakni dosa kesesatanku ditanggung oleh diriku (dan jika aku mendapat petunjuk maka itu adalah disebabkan apa yang diwahyukan Rabbku kepadaku) berupa Alquran dan Hadis Hikmah. (Sesungguhnya Dia Maha Mendengar) Maha mengabulkan doa (lagi Maha Dekat.")
t_id.iwq: Katakanlah: "Jika aku sesat maka sesungguhnya aku sesat atas kemudharatan diriku sendiri; dan jika aku mendapat petunjuk maka itu adalah disebabkan apa yang diwahyukan Tuhanku kepadaku. Sesungguhnya Dia Maha Mendengar lagi Maha Dekat".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قل إن ضللت» عن الحق «فإنما أضل على نفسي» أي إثم ضلالي عليها «وإن اهتديت فيما يوحي إليَّ ربي» من القرآن والحكمة «إنه سميع» للدعاء «قريب»
tafsir.siraj-ar: قُلۡ إِن ضَلَلۡتُ فَإِنَّمَآ أَضِلُّ عَلَىٰ نَفۡسِيۖ وَإِنِ ٱهۡتَدَيۡتُ فَبِمَا يُوحِيٓ إِلَيَّ رَبِّيٓۚ إِنَّهُۥ سَمِيعٞ قَرِيبٞ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah! "Jika aku sesat) dari kebenaran (maka sesungguhnya aku sesat atas kemudaratan diriku sendiri) yakni dosa kesesatanku ditanggung oleh diriku (dan jika aku mendapat petunjuk maka itu adalah disebabkan apa yang diwahyukan Rabbku kepadaku) berupa Alquran dan Hadis Hikmah. (Sesungguhnya Dia Maha Mendengar) Maha mengabulkan doa (lagi Maha Dekat)

ar: وَلَوۡ تَرَىٰۤ إِذۡ فَزِعُوا۟ فَلَا فَوۡتَ وَأُخِذُوا۟ مِن مَّكَانࣲ قَرِیبࣲ
ar_simple: ولو ترى إذ فزعوا فلا فوت وأخذوا من مكان قریب

t.sahih-int: And if you could see when they are terrified but there is no escape, and they will be seized from a place nearby
t.asad: IF THOU couldst but see [how the deniers of the truth will fare on Resurrection Day,] when they will shrink in terror, with nowhere to escape - since they will have been seized from so close nearby
t.yusuf-ali: If thou couldst but see when they will quake with terror; but then there will be no escape (for them), and they will be seized from a position (quite) near
t.pickthall: Couldst thou but see when they are terrified with no escape, and are seized from near at hand
t.arberry: Ah, if thou couldst see when they are terrified, and there is no escape, and they are seized from a place near at hand
t.hilali-khan: And if you could but see, when they will be terrified with no escape (for them), and they will be seized from a near place
t.itani: If you could only see when they are terrified, and there is no escape, and they are seized from a nearby place.
t.mubarakpuri: And if you could but see, when they will be terrified with no escape, and they will be seized from a near place.
t.qarib: If only you could see when they are terrified, but there will be no escape, and they are seized from a place nearby
t.wahiduddin: If you could only see when those who denied the truth are terrified, and there is no way out, and they are seized from a place nearby
t.shakir: And could you see when they shall become terrified, but (then) there shall be no escape and they shall be seized upon from a near place
t.haleem: [Prophet], if you could only see their terror! There will be no escape when they are seized from a nearby place
t.maariful: And if you could only see when they will be terrified! Then there will be no escape, and they will be seized from a place near at hand
t.khattab: If only you could see when they will be horrified with no escape ˹on Judgment Day˺! And they will be seized from a nearby place
t.ghali: And if you could see (them) as they get alarmed; so there is no eluding, (i.e., there is no escaping) and they are taken (away) from a place near (at hand)
t.irving: If you could see when they are startled and there is no escape, and they are seized from a place near at hand
t.iwq: And if you could see when they are terrified but there is no escape, and they will be seized from a place nearby

t_id.kemenag: Dan (alangkah hebatnya) jikalau kamu melihat ketika mereka (orang-orang kafir) terperanjat ketakutan (pada hari kiamat); maka mereka tidak dapat melepaskan diri dan mereka ditangkap dari tempat yang dekat (untuk dibawa ke neraka),
t_id.sabiq: Jika kamu melihat, wahai orang yang melihat dengan cermat, tatkala orang-orang kafir itu ketakutan di saat kebenaran datang, mereka tidak mendapatkan tempat pelarian lagi. Lalu mereka digiring ke neraka dari tempat yang dekat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan jika kamu melihat) hai Muhammad (ketika mereka terperanjat ketakutan) sewaktu mereka dibangkitkan, niscaya kamu akan melihat perkara yang hebat (maka mereka tidak dapat melepaskan diri) dari kekuasaan Kami yang dimaksud adalah dari azab Kami (dan mereka ditangkap dari tempat yang dekat) yaitu dari kubur mereka secara langsung.
t_id.iwq: Dan (alangkah hebatnya) jikalau kamu melihat ketika mereka (orang-orang kafir) terperanjat ketakutan (pada hari kiamat); maka mereka tidak dapat melepaskan diri dan mereka ditangkap dari tempat yang dekat (untuk dibawa ke neraka),

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولوْ ترى» يا محمد «إذْ فزعوا» عند البعث لرأيت أمرا عظيما «فلا فوت» لهم منا، أي لا يفوتوننا «وأخذوا من مكان قريب» أي القبور
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَوۡ تَرَىٰٓ إِذۡ فَزِعُواْ فَلَا فَوۡتَ وَأُخِذُواْ مِن مَّكَانٖ قَرِيبٖ (فَزِعُوا: خَافُوا عِنْدَ مُعَايَنَةِ العَذَابِ, فَلَا فَوْتَ: فَلَا نَجَاةَ لَهُمْ، وَلَا مَهْرَبَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan jika kamu melihat) hai Muhammad (ketika mereka terperanjat ketakutan) sewaktu mereka dibangkitkan, niscaya kamu akan melihat perkara yang hebat (maka mereka tidak dapat melepaskan diri) dari kekuasaan Kami yang dimaksud adalah dari azab Kami (dan mereka ditangkap dari tempat yang dekat) yaitu dari kubur mereka secara langsung

ar: وَقَالُوۤا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ وَأَنَّىٰ لَهُمُ ٱلتَّنَاوُشُ مِن مَّكَانِۭ بَعِیدࣲ
ar_simple: وقالوا ءامنا به وأنى لهم ٱلتناوش من مكان بعید

t.sahih-int: And they will [then] say, "We believe in it!" But how for them will be the taking [of faith] from a place far away
t.asad: and will cry, “We do [now] believe in it!” But how can they [hope to] attain [to salvation] from so far away
t.yusuf-ali: And they will say, "We do believe (now) in the (Truth)"; but how could they receive (Faith) from a position (so far off)
t.pickthall: And say: We (now) believe therein. But how can they reach (faith) from afar off
t.arberry: and they say, 'We believe in it'; but how can they reach from a place far away
t.hilali-khan: And they will say (in the Hereafter): "We do believe (now);" but how could they receive (Faith and the acceptance of their repentance by Allah) from a place so far off (i.e. to return to the worldly life again)
t.itani: And they say, “We have believed in it.” But how can they attain it from a distant place?
t.mubarakpuri: And they will say: "We do believe (now);" but how could they receive (Tanawush) from a place so far off.
t.qarib: They will [then] say, "We do believe in it!" But how can they reach it from such a distant place
t.wahiduddin: then they will say, "We now believe in Him." But how will they attain to faith, having gone so far away from it
t.shakir: And they shall say: We believe in it. And how shall the attaining (of faith) be possible to them from a distant place
t.haleem: they will say, ‘Now we believe in it,’ but how can they reach it from such a distant place
t.maariful: And they will say, “We believe in Him.” And how can they grasp at it (the faith) from a place (so) far off
t.khattab: They will ˹then˺ cry, “We do ˹now˺ believe in it ˹all˺.” But how could they ˹possibly˺ attain faith from a place so far-off ˹from the world˺
t.ghali: And they say, "We (now) believe in it." And however can they succeed in the effort to achieve (belief) from a place far (away)
t.irving: how they will say: "We believe in it!" Yet have they any way to snatch it up at such a distance
t.iwq: And they will then say, "We believe in it!" But how for them will be the taking of faith from a place far away

t_id.kemenag: dan (di waktu itu) mereka berkata: "Kami beriman kepada Allah", bagaimanakah mereka dapat mencapai (keimanan) dari tempat yang jauh itu.
t_id.sabiq: Di saat menyaksikan azab, mereka berkata, "Kami beriman kepada kebenaran itu." Apakah semudah itu mereka mengucapkan keimanan, padahal mereka kini berada di tempat yang jauh dari dunia--sebagai tempat diterimanya keimanan--yang telah berlalu masanya?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan di waktu itu mereka berkata, "Kami beriman kepada-Nya) yakni kepada Nabi Muhammad, atau kepada Alquran (bagaimanakah mereka dapat mencapai) meraih keimanan; dapat dibaca Tanaawusy atau huruf Wau diganti menjadi Hamzah, sehingga bacaannya menjadi Tanaa-usy maksudnya amat mustahillah mereka dapat mencapai keimanan (dari tempat yang jauh itu") dari tempatnya yang sekarang, karena mereka sekarang berada di alam akhirat dan tempat keimanan itu ada di dunia.
t_id.iwq: dan (di waktu itu) mereka berkata: "Kami beriman kepada Allah", bagaimanakah mereka dapat mencapai (keimanan) dari tempat yang jauh itu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقالوا آمنا به» بمحمد أو القرآن «وأنَّى لهم التناوش» بواو وبالهمزة بدلها، أي تناول الإيمان «من مكان بعيد» عن محله إذ هم في الآخرة ومحله الدنيا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَالُوٓاْ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ وَأَنَّىٰ لَهُمُ ٱلتَّنَاوُشُ مِن مَّكَانِۭ بَعِيدٖ (وَأَنَّى لَهُمُ التَّنَاوُشُ: كَيْفَ لَهُمْ تَنَاوُلُ الإِيمَانِ، وَهُمْ فيِ الآخِرَةِ؟)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan di waktu itu mereka berkata, "Kami beriman kepada-Nya) yakni kepada Nabi Muhammad, atau kepada Alquran (bagaimanakah mereka dapat mencapai) meraih keimanan; dapat dibaca Tanaawusy atau huruf Wau diganti menjadi Hamzah, sehingga bacaannya menjadi Tanaa-usy maksudnya amat mustahillah mereka dapat mencapai keimanan (dari tempat yang jauh itu") dari tempatnya yang sekarang, karena mereka sekarang berada di alam akhirat dan tempat keimanan itu ada di dunia

ar: وَقَدۡ كَفَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ مِن قَبۡلُۖ وَیَقۡذِفُونَ بِٱلۡغَیۡبِ مِن مَّكَانِۭ بَعِیدࣲ
ar_simple: وقد كفروا به من قبل ویقذفون بٱلغیب من مكان بعید

t.sahih-int: And they had already disbelieved in it before and would assault the unseen from a place far away
t.asad: seeing that aforetime they had been bent on denying the truth, and had been wont to cast scorn, from far away, on something that was beyond the reach of human perception
t.yusuf-ali: Seeing that they did reject Faith (entirely) before, and that they (continually) cast (slanders) on the unseen from a position far off
t.pickthall: When they disbelieved in it of yore. They aim at the unseen from afar off
t.arberry: seeing they disbelieved in it before, guessing at the Unseen from a place far away
t.hilali-khan: Indeed they did disbelieve (in the Oneness of Allah, Islam, the Quran and Muhammad SAW) before (in this world), and they (used to) conjecture about the unseen [i.e. the Hereafter, Hell, Paradise, Resurrection and the Promise of Allah, etc. (by saying) all that is untrue], from a far place
t.itani: They have rejected it in the past, and made allegations from a far-off place.
t.mubarakpuri: Indeed they did disbelieve before (in this world), and they (used to) conjecture about the Unseen, from a far place.
t.qarib: They had already denied the truth and were prone to guessing from afar about things beyond their perception
t.wahiduddin: They had rejected it before, while they indulged in conjectures from far away
t.shakir: And they disbelieved in it before, and they utter conjectures with regard to the unseen from a distant place
t.haleem: they denied it all in the past, and threw conjecture from a faroff place
t.maariful: while they had rejected it before, and used to make conjectures from a remote place
t.khattab: while they had already rejected it before, guessing blindly from a place ˹equally˺ far-away ˹from the Hereafter˺
t.ghali: And they already disbelieved in it earlier, and they hurl (their guesses) at the Unseen from a place far (away)
t.irving: They have already disbelieved in it, and aimed at [knowing] the Unseen from a distance
t.iwq: And they had already disbelieved in it before and would assault the unseen from a place far away

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah mengingkari Allah sebelum itu; dan mereka menduga-duga tentang yang ghaib dari tempat yang jauh.
t_id.sabiq: Sebelum datangnya hari kiamat ini, mereka sungguh telah mengingkari kebenaran. Mereka pun selalu melontarkan praduga-praduga batil yang jauh dari kebenaran.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah mengingkari Allah sebelum itu) sewaktu mereka hidup di dunia (dan mereka menduga-duga) meramal-ramal (tentang yang gaib dari tempat yang jauh) yaitu terhadap hal-hal yang tidak dapat dijangkau oleh pengetahuan mereka, karena mereka sewaktu di dunia mengatakan terhadap Nabi, bahwa Dia adalah penyihir, tukang syair dan tukang ramal. Mereka mengatakan tentang Alquran, bahwa itu adalah sihir, syair, dan ramalan.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah mengingkari Allah sebelum itu; dan mereka menduga-duga tentang yang ghaib dari tempat yang jauh.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقد كفروا به من قبل» في الدنيا «ويقذفون» يرمون «بالغيب من مكان بعيد» أي بما غاب علمه عنهم غيبة بعيدة حيث قالوا في النبي ساحر، شاعر، كاهن، وفي القرآن سحر، شعر، كهانة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَدۡ كَفَرُواْ بِهِۦ مِن قَبۡلُۖ وَيَقۡذِفُونَ بِٱلۡغَيۡبِ مِن مَّكَانِۭ بَعِيدٖ (وَيَقْذِفُونَ بِالْغَيْبِ: يَرْمُونَ بِالظُّنُونِ الكَاذِبَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah mengingkari Allah sebelum itu) sewaktu mereka hidup di dunia (dan mereka menduga-duga) meramal-ramal (tentang yang gaib dari tempat yang jauh) yaitu terhadap hal-hal yang tidak dapat dijangkau oleh pengetahuan mereka, karena mereka sewaktu di dunia mengatakan terhadap Nabi, bahwa Dia adalah penyihir, tukang syair dan tukang ramal. Mereka mengatakan tentang Alquran, bahwa itu adalah sihir, syair, dan ramalan

ar: وَحِیلَ بَیۡنَهُمۡ وَبَیۡنَ مَا یَشۡتَهُونَ كَمَا فُعِلَ بِأَشۡیَاعِهِم مِّن قَبۡلُۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُوا۟ فِی شَكࣲّ مُّرِیبِۭ
ar_simple: وحیل بینهم وبین ما یشتهون كما فعل بأشیاعهم من قبل إنهم كانوا فی شك مریب

t.sahih-int: And prevention will be placed between them and what they desire, as was done with their kind before. Indeed, they were in disquieting denial
t.asad: And so, a barrier will be set between them and all that they had [ever] desired, as will be done to such of their kind as lived before their time: for, behold, they [too] were lost in doubt amounting to suspicion
t.yusuf-ali: And between them and their desires, is placed a barrier, as was done in the past with their partisans: for they were indeed in suspicious (disquieting) doubt
t.pickthall: And a gulf is set between them and that which they desire, as was done for people of their kind of old. Lo! they were in hopeless doubt
t.arberry: And a barrier is set between them and that they desire, as was done with the likes of them aforetime; they were in doubt disquieting
t.hilali-khan: And a barrier will be set between them and that which they desire [i.e. At-Taubah (turning to Allah in repentance) and the accepting of Faith etc.], as was done in the past with the people of their kind. Verily, they have been in grave doubt
t.itani: A barrier will be placed between them and what they desire, as was done formerly with their counterparts. They were in disturbing doubt.
t.mubarakpuri: And a barrier will be set between them and that which they desire, as was done in the past with the people of their kind. Verily, they have been in grave doubt.
t.qarib: A barrier will be placed between them and what they desire, as was done with their kind before, for they too were in hopeless doubt
t.wahiduddin: And between them and their desires a barrier shall be placed as was done in the past with people of their ilk; for they were indeed in disquieting doubt
t.shakir: And a barrier shall be placed between them and that which they desire, as was done with the likes of them before: surely they are in a disquieting doubt
t.haleem: when a barrier has been placed between them and what they desire, just as was done with their kind before? They were deep in doubt and suspicion
t.maariful: And a barrier will be placed between them and that which they desire, as it will be done with the people of their kind who were before (them). They have been in a perplexing doubt
t.khattab: They will be sealed off from whatever they desire, as was done to their counterparts before. Indeed, they were ˹all˺ in alarming doubt
t.ghali: And a barrier was interposed between them and what they desire (i.e., repentance) as was performed to sects similar to them earlier; surely they were in doubt, causing suspicion
t.irving: Something will intervene between them and whatever they crave to have, just as was done with their [different] sects long before. They have [lived] in such suspicious doubt
t.iwq: And prevention will be placed between them and what they desire, as was done with their kind before. Indeed, they were in disquieting denial

t_id.kemenag: Dan dihalangi antara mereka dengan apa yang mereka ingini sebagaimana yang dilakukan terhadap orang-orang yang serupa dengan mereka pada masa dahulu. Sesungguhnya mereka dahulu (di dunia) dalam keraguan yang mendalam.
t_id.sabiq: Sebagaimana nasib orang-orang sebelum mereka yang menyatakan keimanan setelah lewat masanya, keinginan mereka untuk memanfaatkan keimanan itu pun kini terhalang sudah. Mereka semua adalah orang-orang yang meragukan kebenaran dan kini duduk sebagai pesakitan (terdakwa).
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan dihalangi antara mereka dengan apa yang mereka ingini) yakni ingin beriman, maksudnya iman mereka tidak diterima lagi, karena waktunya sudah habis (sebagaimana dilakukan terhadap orang-orang yang serupa dengan mereka) yakni golongan-golongan mereka dalam hal kekafiran (pada masa dahulu) sebelum mereka. (Sesungguhnya mereka dahulu di dunia dalam keraguan yang mendalam) tentang hal-hal yang sekarang mereka imani; disebabkan sewaktu di dunia mereka tidak mau menganggap dalil-dalil yang menunjuk ke arahnya.
t_id.iwq: Dan dihalangi antara mereka dengan apa yang mereka ingini sebagaimana yang dilakukan terhadap orang-orang yang serupa dengan mereka pada masa dahulu. Sesungguhnya mereka dahulu (di dunia) dalam keraguan yang mendalam.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وحيلَ بينهم وبين ما يشتهون» من الإيمان، أي قبوله «كما فُعل بأشياعهم» أشباههم في الكفر «من قبل» أي قبلهم «إنهم كانوا في شك مريب» موقع في الريبة لهم فيما آمنوا به الآن ولم يعتدوّا بدلائله في الدنيا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَحِيلَ بَيۡنَهُمۡ وَبَيۡنَ مَا يَشۡتَهُونَ كَمَا فُعِلَ بِأَشۡيَاعِهِم مِّن قَبۡلُۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ فِي شَكّٖ مُّرِيبِۭ (بِأَشْيَاعِهِم: أَمْثَالِهِمْ مِنْ كُفَّارِ الأُمَمِ السَّابِقَةِ, مُّرِيبٍ: مُحْدِثٍ لِلرِّيبَةِ وَالقَلَقِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan dihalangi antara mereka dengan apa yang mereka ingini) yakni ingin beriman, maksudnya iman mereka tidak diterima lagi, karena waktunya sudah habis (sebagaimana dilakukan terhadap orang-orang yang serupa dengan mereka) yakni golongan-golongan mereka dalam hal kekafiran (pada masa dahulu) sebelum mereka. (Sesungguhnya mereka dahulu di dunia dalam keraguan yang mendalam) tentang hal-hal yang sekarang mereka imani; disebabkan sewaktu di dunia mereka tidak mau menganggap dalil-dalil yang menunjuk ke arahnya
